topics.title: Overview / Purpose
topics.description: What Requests is, why it exists as an HTTP-for-humans library, and how the top-level package is organized around simple calls, sessions, models, transport, auth, cookies, and utilities.

topics.title: Setup and Installation
topics.description: How Requests is installed and developed, supported Python versions, package metadata, optional extras, testing commands, and documentation build inputs.

topics.title: Public API Interface
topics.description: The top-level request helpers, exported classes and exceptions, public API docs, and the relationship between convenience functions and Session-backed execution.
evidence_pack_hint: docs/api.rst main interface and developer interface sections

topics.title: Request Preparation and Response Objects
topics.description: How user-created requests become prepared requests, how parameters, bodies, files, headers, hooks, cookies, and response content are represented.
evidence_pack_hint: src/requests/models.py Request, PreparedRequest, Response, RequestEncodingMixin, RequestHooksMixin

topics.title: Sessions, Redirects, and State
topics.description: How Session persists defaults, prepares and sends requests, merges settings, follows redirects, rewrites auth/proxies, records history, and mounts adapters.
evidence_pack_hint: docs/user/advanced.rst session objects and prepared requests

topics.title: Transport Adapters, TLS, and Proxies
topics.description: How Requests delegates network I/O to adapters and urllib3, configures pools and retries, handles TLS verification and client certificates, and chooses proxy managers.
evidence_pack_hint: docs/user/advanced.rst SSL cert verification and proxies

topics.title: Authentication, Cookies, and Hooks
topics.description: How Requests applies basic, proxy, digest, and custom authentication, bridges CookieJar behavior into requests, persists cookies, and dispatches response hooks.

topics.title: Utilities, Exceptions, and Data Structures
topics.description: How utility helpers handle encoding, URLs, proxies, headers, environment integration, JSON guessing, rewindability, case-insensitive headers, status codes, and the exception hierarchy.
